articles.title: Muslim women in Juba call for policy inclusion and education access
articles.summary: Muslim women in Juba are requesting inclusion in decision-making processes and improved access to education. During a dialogue held yesterday, participants sought opportunities to contribute to the development of the family bill and other national policies. The event, organized by Women for Justice and Equality, focused on expanding gender-based violence (GBV) response efforts to underrepresented […] The post Muslim women in Juba call for policy inclusion and education access first appeared on Eye Radio .
articles.context: In South Sudan, where ongoing conflict since its independence in 2011 has exacerbated gender inequalities, Muslim women face significant barriers to participation in governance and access to education. The 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan emphasized the need for inclusivity in decision-making, yet marginalized groups, including Muslim women, continue to be underrepresented. This dialogue, held on October 23, 2023, reflects their urgent call for policy inclusion amid persistent gender-based violence.

articles.title: Live blog: President Ruto leads 61st Labour day celebrations in Vihiga
articles.summary: Fete held at Chavakali Boys High School.
articles.context: The 61st Labour Day celebrations in Vihiga come amid ongoing discussions about labor rights and economic recovery in Kenya, particularly following the COVID-19 pandemic's impact on employment. President William Ruto's leadership is under scrutiny as he navigates rising inflation and public discontent, particularly after the controversial Finance Act of 2023, which has faced backlash from various labor unions. This event also serves as a platform for Ruto to reinforce his commitment to workers' rights amid these challenges.

articles.title: Trump administration rejects women picked for soya board, appoints men instead
articles.summary: Push reshapes agricultural leadership and disrupts long-standing nomination norms.
articles.context: The Trump administration's decision to appoint men to the Soya Board in Kenya comes amid ongoing efforts to promote gender equality in agricultural leadership, particularly following the 2010 Constitution that mandates at least one-third of public appointments be women. This move contradicts the progressive strides made by organizations like the Kenya Women in Agriculture and Environment (KWAE), which have advocated for women's representation in decision-making roles since their establishment in 2015.

articles.title: Who is Dr. Labanya Margaret Mathya Ugila, S. Sudan’s new Trade Minister?
articles.summary: Dr. Labanya Margaret Mathya Ugila is a South Sudanese government official with more than three decades of experience in public service, administration and national development. Her appointment as Minister of Trade and Industry comes at a time when the country is seeking to strengthen its economy, promote diversification and attract investment. Dr. Labanya has a […] The post Who is Dr. Labanya Margaret Mathya Ugila, S. Sudan’s new Trade Minister? first appeared on Eye Radio .
articles.context: Dr. Labanya Margaret Mathya Ugila's appointment as South Sudan's Minister of Trade and Industry comes amid ongoing economic challenges exacerbated by the civil conflict that erupted in 2013, leading to a fragile peace agreement in 2018. As the government seeks to implement the Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan, her role is crucial for fostering economic diversification and attracting foreign investment to rebuild the war-torn economy.

articles.title: President Ruto leads 61st Labour day celebrations in Vihiga
articles.context: President William Ruto's participation in the 61st Labour Day celebrations in Vihiga comes at a time of heightened political tension in Kenya, following the contentious 2022 elections and ongoing debates over the cost of living and labor rights. The recent formation of the Kenya Kwanza coalition has also shifted power dynamics, with Ruto seeking to consolidate support from trade unions and workers amid calls for economic reforms and increased job opportunities.

articles.title: Eldoret rolls out hero’s welcome for marathon record breaker Sabastian Sawe
articles.summary: The 30-year-old arrived at Eldoret aboard a Kenya Air Force C-145 Skytruck.
articles.context: Eldoret, a city in Kenya's Rift Valley, has a rich history in long-distance running, producing numerous world champions. The recent success of Sabastian Sawe, who broke the marathon record, comes at a time when Kenya is striving to maintain its dominance in athletics amid increasing competition from emerging nations. This achievement also serves as a morale booster for the country, which has faced challenges such as political unrest and economic pressures in recent years.

articles.title: GIZ distributes goats to households in Yambio and Nzara
articles.summary: The German Development Agency (GIZ) South Sudan, through the Food and Nutrition Security and Management of Natural Resources (FONA) project, has successfully procured and distributed 750 female goats to vulnerable households in Yambio and Nzara counties. The project implemented by Deutsche Gesellschaft für Internationale Zusammenarbeit (GIZ) GmbH is funded by the Federal Ministry for Economic […] The post GIZ distributes goats to households in Yambio and Nzara first appeared on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The distribution of goats by GIZ comes amid ongoing economic challenges and food insecurity in South Sudan, exacerbated by the civil conflict that began in 2013. The 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan aimed to stabilize the region, but implementation has been slow, leaving many communities in Yambio and Nzara vulnerable. This initiative is crucial for enhancing livelihoods and supporting the recovery of households affected by years of instability.

articles.title: US official says Iran war truce 'terminated' hostilities for war powers deadline
articles.summary: Ceasefire claim clouds looming US war powers deadline as legal dispute brews in Congress.
articles.context: The U.S. has been involved in complex negotiations regarding Iran's influence in the Middle East, particularly following the 2015 Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action (JCPOA) and its subsequent withdrawal in 2018. As tensions escalated, the recent ceasefire announcement comes amid a looming deadline for Congress to address war powers related to military actions in the region, particularly as bipartisan disagreements over U.S. military engagement continue to intensify.

articles.title: CES Cabinet approves mapping of recreational centers in Juba
articles.summary: The Central Equatoria State Council of Ministers has approved a plan to map recreational centres and playgrounds in Juba as part of efforts to promote sporting activities. According to a post on the Facebook page of the Office of the Governor, the decision was reached during a council meeting chaired by Governor Emmanuel Adil Anthony […] The post CES Cabinet approves mapping of recreational centers in Juba first appeared on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The approval to map recreational centers in Juba comes amid ongoing efforts to rebuild South Sudan's war-torn infrastructure following the 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan. With the country still grappling with economic challenges and social unrest, promoting recreational activities is seen as a vital step towards fostering community cohesion and improving the quality of life for citizens, particularly among the youth affected by years of conflict.

articles.title: Kenya pushes mining reforms amid illegal operations, fraud concerns
articles.summary: CS Joho says artisanal mining in parts of the country has drifted far from its legal definition.
articles.context: Kenya's mining sector has faced significant challenges, particularly since the 2016 Mining Act aimed to regulate artisanal mining. However, illegal operations have surged, leading to concerns over fraud and environmental degradation. The recent push for reforms by Cabinet Secretary Joho comes amid rising tensions between local communities, who rely on artisanal mining for livelihoods, and government efforts to enforce regulations, highlighting the need for a balance between economic development and legal compliance.

articles.title: US imposes sanctions on former Congo President Joseph Kabila
articles.summary: Kabila cited for his support of Rwanda-backed M23 rebels for fueling political instability.
articles.context: Former President Joseph Kabila, who ruled the Democratic Republic of Congo from 2001 to 2019, has faced accusations of colluding with the M23 rebel group, which has been linked to Rwanda. The M23, which resurfaced in 2021, has exacerbated tensions in eastern Congo, leading to a humanitarian crisis. The U.S. sanctions come amid ongoing diplomatic efforts, including the Nairobi Process initiated in 2022, aimed at stabilizing the region and addressing the influence of foreign actors.

articles.title: Terekeka, Mundri return 41 stolen cattle as peace efforts take hold
articles.summary: Authorities in Terekeka County and Greater Mundri have handed over a total of 41 stolen cattle as part of ongoing efforts to recover livestock raided during recent intercommunal conflict. Officials said 11 cattle were returned in Terekeka, while 30 were handed over by authorities in Greater Mundri to Central and Western Equatoria State officials. The […] The post Terekeka, Mundri return 41 stolen cattle as peace efforts take hold first appeared on Eye Radio .
articles.context: The return of the 41 stolen cattle comes amid ongoing peace efforts following a series of violent clashes between communities in South Sudan's Central and Western Equatoria regions. These conflicts have been exacerbated by historical grievances and competition over resources, particularly since the 2013 civil war. The recent peace agreement signed in 2022 has prompted local authorities to take proactive measures in restoring trust and stability among affected communities.

articles.title: مسؤول رفيع في إدارة ترامب: “الهدنة مع إيران أنهت الأعمال القتالية”، وطهران تقول إن واشنطن ليست جادة في رفع العقوبات
articles.summary: تغطية مباشرة قبل 45 دقيقة عاجل , مسؤول أمريكي: الهدنة مع إيران أنهت الأعمال القتالية أعلن مسؤول رفيع في إدارة الرئيس دونالد ترامب، يوم الخميس، أن وقف إطلاق النار بين الولايات المتحدة وإيران، الذي بدأ مطلع أبريل/نيسان، قد أنهى الأعمال القتالية بين الجانبين، مع اقتراب الموعد النهائي المحدد من قبل الكونغرس بشأن صلاحيات الحرب. وتنقضي The post مسؤول رفيع في إدارة ترامب: “الهدنة مع إيران أنهت الأعمال القتالية”، وطهران تقول إن واشنطن ليست جادة في رفع العقوبات appeared first on صحيفة الراكوبة .
articles.context: تأتي هذه التصريحات في وقت حساس حيث تتصاعد التوترات بين الولايات المتحدة وإيران منذ انسحاب إدارة ترامب من الاتفاق النووي في مايو 2018. الهدنة التي بدأت في أبريل 2023 تأتي في ظل ضغوط من الكونغرس الأمريكي بشأن صلاحيات الحرب، مما يعكس التحديات السياسية الداخلية التي تواجهها إدارة بايدن. في الوقت نفسه، تواصل إيران التأكيد على عدم جدية واشنطن في رفع العقوبات المفروضة عليها، مما يزيد من تعقيد العلاقات بين الطرفين.

articles.title: “تحالف أسطول الصمود” لبي بي سي: ‏”ماضون في الطريق إلى غزة”
articles.summary: "تحالف أسطول الصمود" لبي بي سي: ‏"ماضون في الطريق إلى غزة" صدر الصورة، Reuters Author, إيثار شلبي Role, بي بي سي نيوز عربي 30 أبريل/ نيسان 2026 مدة القراءة: 4 دقائق "المعنويات على متن السفينة مرتفعة، ونحن مصممون على الاستمرار في بذل ما بوسعنا لدعم إنهاء الحصار غير القانوني على غزة"، هكذا تحدث طارق رؤوف The post “تحالف أسطول الصمود” لبي بي سي: ‏”ماضون في الطريق إلى غزة” appeared first on صحيفة الراكوبة .
articles.context: تأتي هذه الأخبار في وقت حساس حيث تصاعدت التوترات في غزة بعد الهجمات الإسرائيلية في عام 2023، والتي أدت إلى تفاقم الأوضاع الإنسانية. "تحالف أسطول الصمود" هو مجموعة من الناشطين الذين يسعون إلى كسر الحصار المفروض على غزة منذ عام 2007. في ظل هذه الظروف، يعتبر دعمهم للجهود الإنسانية خطوة مهمة في سياق الصراع المستمر بين إسرائيل وحماس، والذي شهد تصعيدًا ملحوظًا في السنوات الأخيرة.

articles.title: Uganda’s Bamasaba King visits Kenya to strengthen cross-border cultural ties
articles.summary: Umukuka Jude Mike Mudoma begins two-day tour of Trans Nzoia and Bungoma.
articles.context: The Bamasaba people, straddling the Uganda-Kenya border, have a rich cultural heritage that has historically fostered cross-border ties. Recent tensions over land disputes and political representation in both countries have prompted leaders like Umukuka Jude Mike Mudoma to strengthen cultural connections. His visit comes amid ongoing discussions about the East African Community's efforts to enhance regional integration, particularly following the signing of the EAC Treaty in 2000, which aims to promote cooperation among member states.

articles.title: Residential landlords targeted in KRA plan for mandatory tax registration
articles.context: In Kenya, the Kenya Revenue Authority (KRA) has intensified efforts to enhance tax compliance among landlords, a move that follows the 2021 Finance Act aimed at broadening the tax base. This initiative comes amid ongoing economic challenges exacerbated by the COVID-19 pandemic and rising living costs, prompting the government to seek additional revenue sources. The push for mandatory tax registration reflects a broader strategy to formalize the real estate sector and curb tax evasion.

articles.title: استعدوا لـ”الانهيار العظيم”.. علماء يتوقعون موعد نهاية الكون
articles.summary: يستكشف العلماء سيناريوهات جديدة لمصير الكون، تشير إلى احتمال أن تكون نهايته أقرب مما كان يعتقد سابقا، وذلك استنادا إلى نماذج حديثة وبيانات فلكية متطورة. فقد تنبأت دراسة أجراها علماء من مركز دونستيا الدولي للفيزياء، بأن الكون قد يصل إلى نهايته بعد نحو 33.3 مليار سنة من الانفجار العظيم. وبما أن عمر الكون الحالي يقدّر …
articles.context: تأتي هذه التوقعات في سياق اهتمام متزايد بالعلوم والبحث في السودان، حيث يسعى العلماء المحليون إلى تعزيز الفهم العلمي في ظل التحديات السياسية والاقتصادية التي تواجه البلاد. بعد الثورة السودانية في 2019، التي أدت إلى تغييرات كبيرة في النظام، أصبحت هناك حاجة ملحة لدعم البحث العلمي وتعزيز التعليم، مما يجعل هذه الاكتشافات الفلكية ذات أهمية خاصة في تعزيز الهوية العلمية والثقافية للسودان.

articles.title: ⭕️ قوات الجمارك تُسهم في تفويج حجاج بيت الله الحرام عبر ميناء عثمان دقنة بسواكن
articles.summary: في إطار دورها الوطني والإنساني، شاركت قوات الجمارك في عمليات تفويج حجاج بيت الله الحرام عبر ميناء عثمان دقنة بمدينة سواكن، حيث اضطلعت الإدارة العامة لجمارك البحر الأحمر وإدارة جمارك الميناء بمهامها في تسهيل الإجراءات الجمركية وتيسير حركة الحجاج، بما يضمن انسياب الرحلات وفق الضوابط المنظمة وبأعلى درجات الاستعداد. وأوضح اللواء شرطة مجدي مدني الشيخ، …
articles.context: تأتي هذه المبادرة في سياق تعزيز التعاون بين الحكومة السودانية وقوات الجمارك لتسهيل حركة الحجاج، خاصة بعد التوترات السياسية التي شهدتها البلاد منذ 2019. مع اقتراب موسم الحج، تسعى الحكومة إلى تحسين الخدمات اللوجستية في الموانئ، مثل ميناء عثمان دقنة، لضمان انسيابية الرحلات وتوفير الأمان للحجاج، في ظل التحديات الاقتصادية والأمنية التي تواجهها البلاد.

articles.title: Sawe’s day in the sun: When ‘Nation’ platforms looked away from history
articles.summary: Like the proverbial prophet, Sawe received greater honour away from home.
articles.context: The article highlights the recent recognition of Sawe, a prominent figure in Kenya's struggle for justice and representation, amidst ongoing tensions surrounding historical narratives in the country. With the 2010 Constitution aiming to address historical injustices, the political dynamics have shifted, yet many marginalized voices remain overlooked. The recent 2022 elections further intensified debates on representation, making Sawe's acknowledgment outside Kenya particularly poignant as it reflects the nation's ongoing challenges in reconciling its past.

articles.title: Junior school transition suffers as thousands drop out
articles.summary: Revelations come in the backdrop of a chaotic three years in junior school.
articles.context: The significant dropout rates in Kenya's junior schools follow the disruptions caused by the COVID-19 pandemic, which led to school closures from March 2020 until January 2021. The subsequent implementation of the Competency-Based Curriculum (CBC) in 2019 has faced criticism for its lack of adequate resources and training, exacerbating the challenges for students and educators alike. Additionally, ongoing political tensions surrounding the 2022 elections have diverted attention and funding from the education sector.

articles.title: Inside Sawe’s training regime that yielded marathon world record
articles.summary: Behind-the-scenes strategies that played major part in 31-year-old's historic feat in London.
articles.context: Kenya has a rich history of producing world-class long-distance runners, with athletes like Eliud Kipchoge and Brigid Kosgei setting records. The recent success of marathon runner Sawe, who broke the world record in London in 2023, highlights the country's ongoing investment in sports development, particularly amid challenges such as political instability and economic pressures. This achievement comes as Kenya prepares for the upcoming 2024 Paris Olympics, where expectations for medal contention remain high.

articles.title: Beyond the pitch: How a girl from Kilifi wrote her way into the heart of a Matilda
articles.summary: When Rhodah Beja wrote to Alex Chidiac in 2019, she set off a chain of events that ended at...
articles.context: Rhodah Beja's letter to Australian footballer Alex Chidiac in 2019 emerged amidst Kenya's growing focus on women's sports, particularly following the establishment of the Women's Premier League in 2019. This development coincided with the global push for gender equality in sports, highlighted by the 2023 FIFA Women's World Cup, where increased visibility for female athletes has sparked conversations about representation and support for young girls in sports across the Horn of Africa.

articles.title: Blow to Kakuzi as court dismisses bid to directive to cede 3,200 acres to squatters
articles.summary: NLC had directed Kakuzi to hand over the land to squatters.
articles.context: The case involves Kakuzi, a large agricultural company in Kenya, which has faced allegations of land grabbing and human rights abuses against local communities. In 2019, the National Land Commission (NLC) ordered Kakuzi to cede 3,200 acres of land to squatters, a decision rooted in historical injustices linked to colonial land policies. The ongoing struggle for land rights in Kenya, particularly in the Mount Kenya region, highlights the tensions between corporate interests and local communities seeking restitution.

articles.title: Uproar as schools defy rules, slap parents with illegal fees
articles.summary: Parents being forced to pay school fees higher than the official guidelines.
articles.context: In Kenya, the 2018 Basic Education Amendment Act aimed to eliminate illegal school fees and ensure equitable access to education. However, many schools continue to impose unauthorized fees, exacerbating financial burdens on parents amid ongoing economic challenges following the COVID-19 pandemic. This situation has sparked widespread protests, highlighting tensions between the government, which seeks to enforce compliance, and schools that prioritize revenue generation over educational equity.

articles.title: Degree in one hand, placard in the other: the face of Kenya's jobs crisis
articles.summary: Nearly nine out of ten jobs Kenya created were in the informal sector.
articles.context: Kenya's unemployment crisis has intensified since the COVID-19 pandemic, with the Kenya National Bureau of Statistics reporting that the formal sector has struggled to recover. The government's Vision 2030 initiative, launched in 2008, aimed to create a more robust job market, yet the informal sector now accounts for over 80% of employment. The recent political unrest following the 2022 elections has further exacerbated economic instability, leaving many graduates disillusioned and protesting for better job opportunities.

articles.title: Treasury under fire over budget discrepancies, Sh150bn in mystery account
articles.summary: Jimi Wanjigi raises fresh questions over revision of budget figures.
articles.context: The scrutiny over Kenya's budget discrepancies comes amid heightened political tensions following the 2022 general elections, where accusations of corruption and mismanagement have intensified. Jimi Wanjigi, a prominent businessman and political figure, has been vocal about financial transparency, particularly after the controversial 2021 budget revisions that saw significant reallocations. The revelation of Sh150 billion in an unexplained account raises alarms about fiscal accountability, especially as the government grapples with mounting public debt and economic recovery efforts post-COVID-19.

articles.title: بريطانيا ترفع مستوى التهديد الإرهابي بعد وقائع طعن
articles.summary: بريطانيا ترفع مستوى التهديد الإرهابي بعد وقائع طعن صدر الصورة، Metropolitan Police/PA Wire التعليق على الصورة، لقطة من كاميرا شرطي تظهر توقيف رجل بعد طعن شخصين في "غولدرز غرين" شمال غرب لندن، 29 أبريل/نيسان 2026. قبل 2 ساعة مدة القراءة: 3 دقائق رفعت بريطانيا مستوى التهديد الإرهابي على مستوى البلاد من "كبير" إلى "خطير" يوم The post بريطانيا ترفع مستوى التهديد الإرهابي بعد وقائع طعن appeared first on صحيفة الراكوبة .
articles.context: تأتي هذه الزيادة في مستوى التهديد الإرهابي في بريطانيا في وقت حساس، حيث تشهد البلاد تصاعدًا في حوادث العنف المرتبطة بالتطرف. في عام 2023، تم إحباط عدة مخططات إرهابية، مما أثار قلق السلطات بشأن الأمن العام. كما أن التوترات السياسية والاجتماعية في أوروبا، خاصة بعد الأحداث في السودان وتأثيرها على الهجرة، قد ساهمت في تفاقم المخاوف من تهديدات إرهابية محتملة.

articles.title: الدفاعات الجوية الإيرانية تشتبك مع مُسيرات فوق العاصمة طهران، وترامب يقول دمرنا الجيش وقادته
articles.summary: مباشر , الدفاعات الجوية الإيرانية تشتبك مع مُسيرات فوق العاصمة طهران، وترامب يقول دمرنا الجيش وقادته جدد الرئيس الأمريكي دونالد ترامب تصريحه بأن إيران "متلهفة جداً لإبرام اتفاق" مضيفاً أن الحكومة الإيرانية "في وضع مزرٍ للغاية، ولا تملك شيئاً تقريباً، حتى القيادة"، وأنباء عن اشتباك الدفاعات الجوية الإيرانية مع مُسيرات فوق العاصمة طهران ملخص تغطية The post الدفاعات الجوية الإيرانية تشتبك مع مُسيرات فوق العاصمة طهران، وترامب يقول دمرنا الجيش وقادته appeared first on صحيفة الراكوبة .
articles.context: The recent engagement of Iranian air defenses with drones over Tehran comes amid heightened tensions following the U.S. withdrawal from the 2015 nuclear deal in May 2018 and subsequent sanctions that have severely impacted Iran's economy. Trump's remarks reflect ongoing U.S.-Iran hostilities, particularly after the assassination of Iranian General Qassem Soleimani in January 2020, which escalated military confrontations in the region. This incident underscores the fragile security landscape in the Middle East, affecting neighboring countries like Sudan.

articles.title: أروى جودة: بحب الكوارع والممبار.. وزوجي نفسه يشترى جزيرة في أسوان
articles.summary: كشفت الفنانة أروى جودة عن تفاصيل حياتها الزوجية وعلاقتها بزوجها وأبنائه، مؤكدة أن الزواج منحها حالة من الاستقرار والسعادة، وأن زوجها يمثل لها أهم شيء في حياتها. وخلال تصريحات خاصة لبرنامج “الستات ما يعرفوش يكدبوا” الذي تقدمه الإعلاميتان هبة الأباصيري ومها بهنسي، أوضحت أروى أنها حرصت منذ إعلان خطوبتها على مشاركة جمهورها تلك اللحظات المهمة، …
articles.context: The Horn of Africa is currently experiencing a complex interplay of social and political dynamics, particularly in Sudan, where ongoing conflicts and economic challenges have affected many aspects of daily life. Amidst this backdrop, public figures like أروى جودة offer a glimpse into personal narratives that resonate with audiences seeking stability and happiness. Her comments about marriage and family life reflect a desire for normalcy in a region facing significant upheaval, particularly since the 2021 military coup in Sudan.

articles.title: الدولار يتحول إلى “عملة نادرة” في إسرائيل
articles.summary: تواجه مكاتب الصرافة في تل أبيب نقصا حادا في العملات الأجنبية وخاصة الدولار على خلفية الزيادة التاريخية في سعر صرف الشيكل، واضطراب تدفق العملات الأجنبية.ذكر مراسل وكالة “نوفوستي” بعد تجوله في عدة مكاتب صرافة في المدينة بإسرائيل أن العملة الوطنية الإسرائيلية “تواصل تعزيزها السريع مقابل الدولار”، مشيرا إلى انخفاص سعر صرف الدولار في السوق الموازية …
articles.context: تشهد إسرائيل في الآونة الأخيرة ارتفاعًا ملحوظًا في قيمة الشيكل، مما أدى إلى تراجع الطلب على الدولار الأمريكي، خاصة في ظل الأزمات الاقتصادية التي تعاني منها دول الجوار مثل السودان. هذا التغير يأتي في وقت حساس، حيث تتأثر العلاقات الاقتصادية في المنطقة بالتوترات السياسية والنزاعات المستمرة، مثل الصراع في السودان الذي يؤثر على تدفق العملات الأجنبية إلى إسرائيل.

articles.title: UN extends South Sudan mission, cuts troop ceiling

The troop ceiling reduction reflects concerns over the mission's effectiveness and funding challenges.

articles.title: AI in the workplace this Labour Day
articles.summary: The theme of today’s celebrations should be “welcoming AI to the workplace.”
articles.context: As Kenya grapples with high unemployment rates, currently at 5.7% as of 2023, the integration of AI into the workplace presents both opportunities and challenges. The government's push for digital transformation, highlighted in the 2022 National Digital Master Plan, aims to enhance productivity and innovation. However, concerns about job displacement and the need for reskilling workers are critical issues as the country navigates its post-pandemic recovery and economic growth.

articles.title: National consciousness is vital for building prosperous nation
articles.summary: Kenya’s rulers would rather manage a conquered population than govern a conscious one.
articles.context: Kenya has faced significant political turmoil since the contested 2007 elections, which ignited violence and deepened ethnic divisions. The 2010 Constitution aimed to foster national unity and decentralize power, yet ongoing issues with corruption and governance have hindered progress. The recent push for a "Building Bridges Initiative" (BBI) following the 2018 handshake between President Uhuru Kenyatta and opposition leader Raila Odinga underscores the struggle for a cohesive national identity amidst persistent tribalism and political manipulation.

articles.title: Varsity placement key to State scholarships
articles.summary: Eligible students should be guided through the process so that they don't miss out.
articles.context: In Kenya, the government has emphasized the importance of university placement for students seeking State scholarships, particularly following the 2022 Education Act that reformed the scholarship allocation process. With rising youth unemployment and a competitive job market, the need for equitable access to higher education has become critical. The recent 2023 Kenya Certificate of Secondary Education (KCSE) results further underscore the urgency, as thousands of students vie for limited scholarship opportunities to pursue their academic aspirations.

articles.title: Evolving role of corporate foundations
articles.summary: Corporate foundations moving from the margins to centre of development practice.
articles.context: In Kenya, the rise of corporate foundations has gained momentum amid the government's push for public-private partnerships to address development challenges. Following the 2010 Constitution, which emphasized corporate social responsibility, companies like Safaricom and Equity Bank have increasingly engaged in philanthropic initiatives. This shift is particularly significant as the country grapples with economic recovery post-COVID-19 and seeks innovative solutions to persistent issues like poverty and unemployment, making corporate involvement crucial for sustainable development.

articles.title: Let Mandera airstrip crash trigger change
articles.summary: That such an incident occurred at all is unacceptable.
articles.context: The Mandera airstrip crash highlights ongoing safety concerns in Kenya's aviation sector, particularly in remote regions. Mandera County, bordering Somalia, has faced instability due to the presence of Al-Shabaab militants, complicating infrastructure development. The Kenyan government has struggled to balance security and accessibility in these areas, especially following the 2014 Garissa University attack, which underscored the need for improved emergency response capabilities and transport infrastructure in conflict-prone regions.

articles.title: Probe Senate bribery claim on surrogacy Bill
articles.summary: The effectiveness of the lawmakers in serving as a watchdog for the public good remains pivotal.
articles.context: The surrogacy Bill in Kenya has sparked intense debate since its introduction in 2021, with concerns over ethical implications and the potential for exploitation. Recent allegations of bribery among senators during discussions of the Bill have raised alarms about legislative integrity, particularly as the country grapples with a history of corruption in politics. This situation is compounded by the ongoing push for reproductive rights amidst societal and religious opposition, making scrutiny of lawmakers crucial.

articles.title: Fix woes to boost growth
articles.summary: There is a need to review punitive policies such as high taxes and wasteful spending.
articles.context: Kenya's economy has been under strain due to high taxation and misallocation of resources, particularly following the 2018 "Big Four Agenda" aimed at boosting manufacturing, universal healthcare, and affordable housing. The COVID-19 pandemic exacerbated these challenges, leading to increased public debt and economic contraction. As the government seeks to recover and attract foreign investment, addressing these punitive policies is crucial for sustainable growth and stability in the region.

articles.context: Ethiopia has been actively seeking foreign investment to bolster its economy, especially following the Tigray conflict that ended in November 2022, which left the nation in need of reconstruction and development. The Ethiopian government has prioritized infrastructure projects, such as the new airport in Bishoftu, to enhance connectivity and attract tourism. Strengthening ties with South Korea aligns with Ethiopia's broader strategy to diversify its investment sources and leverage technological advancements.

articles.title: Kenyan leader sparks uproar after mocking Nigerians' spoken English
articles.summary: William Ruto says Kenyans speak "some of the best English in the world" while it is hard to understand Nigerians.
articles.context: William Ruto's comments come amid ongoing efforts to strengthen regional ties within the East African Community (EAC) and the African Union, where linguistic diversity is a critical issue. His remarks also reflect Kenya's historical rivalry with Nigeria, particularly in leadership and influence within the African continent. This incident follows Ruto's recent push for enhanced cooperation in trade and security, making his comments particularly sensitive in the context of fostering unity among African nations.

articles.title: ACSO Warns of Strong Legal Action Against Civil Society Organizations Receiving Foreign Currency Illegally
articles.summary: The Authority for Civil Society Organizations (ACSO) has strictly emphasized that any civil society organization is obligated to follow National Bank directives and legal procedures when receiving and utilizing financial support from abroad. In a statement released on April 23, 2026, the Authority announced that “serious legal measures” will be taken against organizations that receive, […]
articles.context: The ACSO's warning comes amid heightened scrutiny of civil society organizations in Ethiopia, particularly following the 2020 Tigray conflict, which has strained relations between the government and NGOs. The 2021 Proclamation on Civil Society Organizations tightened regulations on foreign funding, reflecting the government's concerns over foreign influence and national sovereignty. This latest statement signals an ongoing effort to enforce compliance and control over civil society amid a politically sensitive environment.

articles.title: East African Leaders Convene in Addis to Synchronize Trade and Housing Policies
articles.summary: Ethiopia is currently hosting the inaugural “Addis Africa” Real Estate Exhibition, marking the first East African regional conference of its kind. Opened this Thursday, this dual-purpose program aims to harmonize policy frameworks across the Horn of Africa while positioning Addis Ababa as a premier continental hub for real estate investment. The East African regional conference […]
articles.context: The East African region has faced economic challenges exacerbated by political instability and conflict, particularly in Ethiopia, where the Tigray War (2020-2022) had significant impacts on the economy and infrastructure. The African Union's efforts to mediate peace and promote regional cooperation have gained urgency, especially with the recent signing of the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA) agreement in 2021, which aims to boost intra-African trade and investment.

articles.title: NEBE Reports 50.5 Million Voters Registered Amid Election Strains
articles.summary: Ethiopia’s National Election Board has announced that more than 50 million citizens have registered to vote in the country’s upcoming national election, as officials reveal budget shortfalls and reported disruptions in several regions. The Board said 50,514,155 voters had been registered, including more than 45.1 million through manual processes and the remainder digitally. The figures […]
articles.context: Ethiopia is preparing for its national elections scheduled for June 2024, amid ongoing tensions from the Tigray conflict that erupted in November 2020. The peace agreement signed in November 2022 has not fully stabilized the political landscape, with various regions experiencing unrest and logistical challenges. The National Election Board's report of over 50 million registered voters highlights both the electoral enthusiasm and the significant hurdles posed by budget constraints and regional disruptions.

articles.title: Italy Commits €25mln to Ethiopia Under Mattei Plan
articles.summary: Italy has committed 25 million euros to Ethiopia under its Mattei Plan to support job creation, economic growth, and sustainable development, as Italian companies expand their presence at Big 5 Construct Ethiopia 2026 in Addis Ababa. The announcement comes during the April 23–25 exhibition at the Addis Ababa International Convention Center, where more than 20 […]
articles.context: Italy's commitment to Ethiopia comes amid ongoing challenges in the region, including the aftermath of the Tigray conflict, which officially ended with a peace agreement in November 2022. The Ethiopian government is focused on economic recovery and job creation as it seeks to stabilize the country and rebuild international partnerships. The Mattei Plan aligns with Italy's broader strategy to enhance its influence in Africa, particularly in light of increasing competition from countries like China and Turkey.

articles.title: Amnesty Flags Evictions, Arrests, and Delays in Justice in Ethiopia
articles.summary: Amnesty International has raised concerns over a range of human rights issues in Ethiopia, citing forced evictions, restrictions on civic space, and a lack of accountability for past abuses in its latest global report. The report, titled The State of the World’s Human Rights, presents a global and regional overview alongside country-specific assessments. Amnesty said […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has been grappling with internal conflict since the outbreak of the Tigray War in November 2020, which has exacerbated ethnic tensions and led to widespread human rights violations. The federal government, led by Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed, has faced criticism for its handling of the conflict and for ongoing issues such as forced evictions in regions like Oromia. The recent peace agreement signed in November 2022 has not fully addressed these human rights concerns.

articles.title: تقرير: مرتزقة كولومبيون بتمويل إماراتي ساندوا قوات الدعم السريع
articles.summary: التحقيق، الذي أعدّته مجموعة "كونفليكت إنسايتس غروب" (CIG) المتخصصة في التحليل الأمني، إلى بيانات جمعت عبر تتبّع الهواتف المحمولة للمقاتلين الكولومبيين.
articles.context: تتزامن هذه المعلومات مع تصاعد النزاع في السودان منذ أبريل 2023 بين قوات الدعم السريع والجيش السوداني، مما أدى إلى تفاقم الأوضاع الإنسانية. كما أن الدعم الخارجي، بما في ذلك التمويل الإماراتي، يعكس دور القوى الإقليمية في الصراع، حيث تسعى الإمارات لتعزيز نفوذها في المنطقة. التحقيق يكشف عن استخدام المرتزقة الكولومبيين كجزء من استراتيجية عسكرية معقدة تدعمها قوى خارجية.

articles.title: 'I cried so hard' - the Kenyan WNBA star who beat US visa heartbreak
articles.summary: Madina Okot only started playing basketball six years ago, but the Kenyan's rapid rise has seen her set a WNBA milestone at the age of 21.
articles.context: Madina Okot's journey to the WNBA comes amid Kenya's growing investment in women's sports, highlighted by the 2021 launch of the Women's Premier League, aimed at promoting female athletes. This progress is significant as it contrasts with the historical underrepresentation of women in sports in the region. Additionally, her success follows Kenya's recent efforts to improve international relations and sports diplomacy, particularly after hosting the 2021 World Athletics U20 Championships.

articles.title: Wildberries marks official launch of its e-commerce marketplace in Ethiopia
articles.summary: Wildberries, the international e-commerce giant, has entered Ethiopia by opening its platform to local sellers. Its broader strategy focuses on adapting its global model to the local market. It has confirmed it is working closely with local partners to enable smoother last-mile delivery and payment integration, including enabling transactions in Birr. The company noted that […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's e-commerce landscape has been evolving amid ongoing political and economic challenges, particularly following the Tigray conflict that escalated in late 2020. The government's push for digital transformation and economic recovery has created a favorable environment for foreign investment, particularly in technology and e-commerce. Wildberries' entry aligns with the Ethiopian government's 2021 Digital Ethiopia 2025 strategy, which aims to enhance digital infrastructure and promote local entrepreneurship.

articles.title: UAE-backed Colombian mercenaries provided support to Sudan paramilitary, report says
articles.summary: The Conflict Insights Group (CIG) says its research also shows the extent of UAE involvement.
articles.context: The ongoing conflict in Sudan, particularly the power struggle between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF), has escalated since April 2023. The UAE's involvement, particularly through the recruitment of Colombian mercenaries, highlights its strategic interest in the region, especially following the 2020 normalization agreement with Israel. This support for the RSF raises concerns about the UAE's role in exacerbating instability in Sudan and the broader Horn of Africa.

articles.title: Ethiopian Airlines Firms up Order for Six Boeing 787-9 Dreamliner Jets
articles.summary: Ethiopian Airlines has converted the option to buy six Boeing 787-9 Dreamliner jets into a firm order, according to the US plane maker. Ethiopian and Boeing announced the
articles.context: Ethiopian Airlines, Africa's largest airline, has been pivotal in connecting the continent globally, especially amid the ongoing Tigray conflict that began in November 2020. The airline's expansion with the new Boeing 787-9 jets comes as Ethiopia seeks to revitalize its economy and enhance its international presence following a peace agreement in November 2022. This order underscores the airline's resilience and strategic importance in a region facing significant political and economic challenges.

articles.title: Somali intelligence says over 33 al-Shabaab terrorists killed in Middle Shabelle operation
articles.summary: Coordinated raids disrupted planned attacks by group gathering around 90 fighters, NISA adds
articles.context: The recent operation in Middle Shabelle comes amid heightened tensions following the Somali government's offensive against al-Shabaab, which has intensified since President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud's election in May 2022. The group has been regrouping, with reports indicating they were planning significant attacks ahead of the upcoming elections scheduled for late 2023. This operation underscores the ongoing struggle between Somali National Army forces and al-Shabaab, which has deep roots in the region's political instability and violence.

articles.title: Nearly 4M displaced returned across Sudan amid fragile conditions: UN migration agency
articles.summary: IOM says lack of housing, health care, jobs could make returns unsustainable
articles.context: The return of nearly 4 million displaced individuals in Sudan follows years of conflict exacerbated by the 2019 ousting of President Omar al-Bashir and the subsequent power struggle between military and civilian leaders. The fragile political landscape, marked by the October 2021 coup led by General Abdel Fattah al-Burhan, has hindered stability and recovery efforts, leaving many returnees without adequate housing, healthcare, or employment opportunities. This situation poses significant challenges for the International Organization for Migration (IOM) and humanitarian efforts.

articles.title: Crisis in the Middle East Could Cost Africa 0.2% in Annual Economic Growth
articles.summary: A joint policy document by four regional organizations the crisis in the Middle East having on the African economies, with growth in the continent forecast to decline by
articles.context: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East, particularly the Israel-Palestine tensions that escalated in October 2023, has significant implications for African economies reliant on oil imports and trade routes. Ethiopia, facing its own internal challenges, including the aftermath of the Tigray conflict and ongoing political reforms, is particularly vulnerable to external shocks. The joint policy document from the African Union, IGAD, COMESA, and ECOWAS underscores the interconnectedness of regional stability and economic growth in Africa.

articles.title: Zemen Bank Teams up with EIB Global to Mobilise €40mln in Financing for Agribusinesses
articles.summary: Agricultural businesses in Ethiopia will be eligible for as much as €40 million in financing as a result of an agreement between the European Investment Bank Group’s development
articles.context: Ethiopia's agricultural sector, vital for its economy, has faced challenges due to the ongoing Tigray conflict, which began in November 2020, disrupting supply chains and exacerbating food insecurity. The recent agreement between Zemen Bank and EIB Global, announced in October 2023, aims to revitalize agribusinesses, supporting Ethiopia's recovery and stability amidst political tensions and economic hardships. This financing could bolster local production and enhance food security in a region grappling with conflict-related disruptions.

articles.title: Korir defends Boston Marathon title with course record
articles.summary: Kenya's John Korir sets a new course record of two hours, one minute and 52 seconds to win a second successive Boston Marathon.
articles.context: John Korir's victory at the Boston Marathon comes at a time when Kenya is facing scrutiny over its athletics governance, particularly following the World Athletics' decision to impose stricter regulations on doping. This win not only reinforces Kenya's dominance in long-distance running but also serves as a morale booster for the nation, which has been grappling with the fallout from doping scandals that have marred its reputation in recent years.

articles.title: AU Condemns Israeli Diplomatic Envoy to Somaliland
articles.summary: The African Union (AU) and a dozen governments have condemned Israel’s decision to appoint a diplomatic envoy to Somaliland. “The Union does not recognize Somaliland as an independent state,” reads a statement issued by the AU Commission yesterday in response to Israel’s appointment of its first ambassador to Somaliland last week. On April 15, 2026, […]
articles.context: Somaliland declared independence from Somalia in 1991, but it remains unrecognized by the international community, including the African Union, which views it as part of Somalia. The recent appointment of an Israeli ambassador to Somaliland marks a significant diplomatic shift and has raised tensions, as it challenges the AU's stance and Somalia's claims over Somaliland. This development comes amidst ongoing efforts by Somaliland to gain international recognition and strengthen its diplomatic ties.

articles.title: Ethiopia’s quiet energy shift: Ethio telecom’s role in building a green future
articles.summary: In the global race toward decarbonization, significant changes are often driven not by traditional energy giants but by agile institutions that blend digital ecosystems with physical infrastructure. In East Africa, a quiet yet substantial energy revolution is taking shape, with Ethio telecom at the forefront. The company is rapidly transforming from a conventional connectivity provider […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has been undergoing a significant political transition since the appointment of Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed in 2018, which has included economic reforms and a push for digital transformation. Amid ongoing tensions in the Tigray region and efforts to stabilize the country, Ethio telecom is leveraging its infrastructure to support renewable energy initiatives, aligning with Ethiopia's 2025 Climate Resilient Green Economy Strategy, which aims to achieve carbon neutrality by 2030.

articles.title: EU resumes direct budgetary support to Ethiopia
articles.summary: The European Union has officially resumed its direct budgetary support to Ethiopia, which had been suspended since 2020. This move is regarded as a significant boost to the country’s current reform agenda and marks the beginning of a new chapter in the 50-year strategic partnership between the two entities. The resumption of support will result […]
articles.context: The EU suspended its budgetary support to Ethiopia in 2020 due to concerns over human rights violations amid the Tigray conflict, which erupted in November 2020. The resumption of aid comes after the signing of a peace agreement in November 2022 between the Ethiopian government and Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF), signaling a potential stabilization in the region and a renewed commitment to reforms under Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed's administration.

articles.title: EU Resumes Budgetary Support to Ethiopian Government
articles.summary: The European Union (EU) has announced it has resumed budget support to Ethiopia, more than five years after the bloc cut payments over concerns of human rights violations during the northern Ethiopia war. The EU is starting the budgetary support with a 140 million Euro allocated for priority sectors. Jozeph Sikela, EU commissioner for international […]
articles.context: The EU's decision to resume budgetary support comes after a prolonged suspension initiated in 2017 due to escalating human rights abuses during the Tigray conflict, which erupted in November 2020. The Ethiopian government, led by Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed, signed a peace agreement with the Tigray People's Liberation Front (TPLF) in November 2022, marking a critical step towards stability. This renewed support reflects the EU's recognition of Ethiopia's efforts to rebuild and foster political reconciliation.

articles.title: 'They told me he was dead': Children born near Kenyan army base learn truth about UK soldier dads
articles.summary: A DNA and legal project has identified the fathers of 20 children born near a military base in Kenya.
articles.context: The article highlights the ongoing legacy of the British military presence in Kenya, particularly at the British Army Training Unit Kenya (BATUK) established in 1980. Amid rising tensions over military operations and allegations of human rights abuses, the discovery of these children's paternity raises questions about accountability and the impact of foreign military bases on local communities. This issue is further complicated by Kenya's 2010 Constitution, which emphasizes children's rights and the need for transparency in such matters.

articles.title: school bus quotes
articles.context: In Ethiopia, the ongoing Tigray conflict, which erupted in November 2020, has severely disrupted education and transportation systems. As peace efforts, including the November 2022 Pretoria Agreement, aim to stabilize the region, the safe transport of students remains a pressing concern. Recent incidents involving school buses highlight the challenges faced by families and communities in conflict-affected areas, underscoring the need for reliable and secure educational access amidst ongoing tensions.

articles.title: 'The weapons were loud, but there was always music': Sudanese band play on through the war
articles.summary: One of Sudan's most popular bands, Aswat Almadina, recall being in the studio when the war broke out three years ago.
articles.context: Since the outbreak of conflict in Sudan in April 2023, following a power struggle between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces, the country has faced escalating violence and humanitarian crises. Amidst this turmoil, cultural expressions like music have become vital for resilience and unity. Aswat Almadina, a band that emerged during Sudan's 2019 revolution, symbolizes the enduring spirit of Sudanese youth and their desire for peace and stability in a fractured nation.

articles.title: Bondholder, Commercial Creditor Deals Remain Out of Reach in Ethiopia Debt Saga
articles.summary: The co-chairs of Ethiopia’s official creditors committee (OCC) report the country has yet to ink restructuring agreements with bondholders and commercial creditors that comply with its comparability of treatment (CoT) requirements. The committee, chaired by China and France, rejected an agreement in principle reached between the Ethiopian government and holders of its one billion dollar […]
articles.context: Ethiopia has been grappling with a severe debt crisis exacerbated by the Tigray conflict, which began in November 2020. The government’s efforts to secure debt restructuring have been complicated by the need to adhere to the G20’s Common Framework for Debt Treatments, which emphasizes comparability of treatment among creditors. The ongoing negotiations with bondholders and commercial creditors are critical as Ethiopia seeks to stabilize its economy and regain access to international financial markets.

articles.title: TPLF Central Committee Convenes in Axum as Administrative Rift Deepens
articles.summary: A high stakes meeting of the proscribed Tigray People Liberation Front (TPLF) Central Committee in Axum is intensifying fears of a political rupture in Tigray, with mounting indications that the party could move to reject the region’s interim administration and push to restore its pre-war government structure. The meeting, which began on April 15, 2026, […]
articles.context: The TPLF, once the dominant force in Ethiopia's ruling coalition, has been embroiled in a brutal conflict with the federal government since November 2020, leading to widespread humanitarian crises. The November 2022 Pretoria Agreement aimed to cease hostilities, but tensions remain high as the TPLF's dissatisfaction with the interim administration, established post-conflict, grows. The Axum meeting signals a potential shift towards rejecting the interim governance model and re-establishing TPLF's former authority in Tigray.

articles.title: Education Ministry’s Digital Exam Scheme Triggers Parliamentary Uproar
articles.summary: The Education Ministry has come under intense parliamentary scrutiny over the effectiveness and readiness of its ambitious plan to conduct the upcoming university entrance exam entirely online, set to take place within the next few months. Earlier this year, the Minister of Education announced a landmark shift from paper-based exams to an entirely online modality […]
articles.context: Ethiopia's education sector has faced significant challenges amid ongoing political unrest and conflict, particularly following the Tigray War that began in November 2020. The government's push for digital transformation in education, including the shift to online exams, comes as part of broader reforms aimed at modernizing the system and addressing disparities exacerbated by the conflict. However, concerns about infrastructure and access have intensified scrutiny from parliament, reflecting the fragile state of governance and public trust.

articles.title: UN relief chief warns South Sudan nearing famine as crisis deepens
articles.summary: 'The world’s youngest nation stands at a dangerous crossroads,' Tom Fletcher tells Security Council
articles.context: South Sudan has faced ongoing conflict since its independence from Sudan in 2011, exacerbated by a civil war that erupted in 2013, leading to a humanitarian crisis. The 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan aimed to restore peace but has struggled to be fully implemented, with violence and political instability persisting. As a result, millions are at risk of famine, particularly in regions like Unity State, where access to aid is severely restricted.

articles.title: UN official warns of rising violence, mounting humanitarian crisis in South Sudan
articles.summary: 'Security situation remains deeply concerning ...Civilians continue to bear the brunt,' says head of UN Mission in South Sudan
articles.context: South Sudan has been grappling with civil conflict since its independence from Sudan in 2011, particularly following the outbreak of violence in December 2013 between forces loyal to President Salva Kiir and those aligned with former Vice President Riek Machar. The 2018 Revitalized Agreement on the Resolution of the Conflict in South Sudan aimed to establish peace, but ongoing violence and political instability have hindered its implementation, exacerbating the humanitarian crisis affecting millions.

articles.title: OIC condemns Israeli move to appoint envoy in Somaliland
articles.summary: Organization of Islamic Cooperation denounces Israel’s reported plan to appoint envoy in Somaliland, calling it a violation of Somalia’s sovereignty
articles.context: The Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) has expressed concern over Israel's reported intention to appoint an envoy in Somaliland, a self-declared independent region that Somalia does not recognize. This move comes amid ongoing tensions following the 1991 civil war, which fragmented Somalia and led to Somaliland's unilateral declaration of independence in 1991. The situation is further complicated by the broader geopolitical dynamics in the Horn of Africa, including Somalia's relationships with regional powers and the ongoing quest for international recognition.

articles.title: Somalia says Israeli move on Somaliland violates sovereignty, risks regional stability
articles.summary: State Minister Ali Mohammad Omar says Mogadishu rejects recognition of breakaway region, warns of security risks in Red Sea corridor
articles.context: Somaliland declared independence from Somalia in 1991, but it has not received international recognition, leading to ongoing tensions between Mogadishu and Hargeisa. The recent announcement by Israel to strengthen ties with Somaliland, including potential diplomatic recognition, has heightened concerns in Somalia about the implications for regional stability, particularly in light of the 2020 normalization agreements between Israel and several Arab states. Somalia's government fears this move could embolden separatist sentiments and disrupt security in the strategically vital Red Sea corridor.

articles.title: Somalia condemns Israeli ambassador's appointment to its breakaway region of Somaliland
articles.summary: Mogadishu declares Tel Aviv's move violation of Somalia’s sovereignty, unity, and territorial integrity, urging international bodies to uphold international law, as it clearly contradicts UN Charter, African Union's foundational principles
articles.context: Somaliland declared independence from Somalia in 1991, but it has not received international recognition, leading to ongoing tensions between Mogadishu and Hargeisa. The recent appointment of an Israeli ambassador to Somaliland is seen as a direct challenge to Somalia's sovereignty, especially following the 2020 normalization agreements between Israel and several Arab states, which have shifted regional alliances. Somalia's government, led by President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud since May 2022, emphasizes the need for international support to uphold its territorial integrity.

articles.title: Airstrikes in Somalia kill 54 al-Shabaab terrorists
articles.summary: Operations also targeted supply stockpiles, significantly weakening group’s logistical capacity, mobility, ability to organize attacks, says Defense Ministry
articles.context: The recent airstrikes come amid intensified efforts by the Somali government and African Union forces to combat al-Shabaab, particularly following the 2022 agreement between Somalia and the African Union to bolster military cooperation. The offensive is part of a broader campaign launched in 2021 under President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud, aimed at reclaiming territory and dismantling the group’s operational capabilities, which have been a persistent threat to stability in the region.

articles.title: Israel appoints 1st ambassador to Somalia's breakaway region in controversial move
articles.summary: Move comes after Tel Aviv announced decision to recognize Somaliland in December 2025
articles.context: Somaliland declared independence from Somalia in 1991 but has not been internationally recognized, leading to ongoing tensions between the two. In December 2025, Israel's recognition of Somaliland marked a significant shift in diplomatic relations, as it aligns with Somaliland's aspirations for statehood and could influence other nations' stances. This move is particularly controversial given Somalia's longstanding claim over Somaliland and the fragile political dynamics in the Horn of Africa.

articles.title: UN chief calls Sudan conflict world’s ‘largest humanitarian crisis’ 3 years on
articles.summary: ‘Nearly 34 million people inside Sudan now need humanitarian assistance,’ Antonio Guterres says
articles.context: The conflict in Sudan escalated dramatically in April 2023 when fighting broke out between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces, leading to widespread violence and displacement. This turmoil follows years of political instability after the ousting of President Omar al-Bashir in 2019 and the subsequent struggle for power among military factions. The ongoing crisis has severely hindered humanitarian efforts, exacerbating the needs of millions already facing food insecurity and health challenges.

articles.title: 5 miners killed in gold mine collapse in eastern Sudan, medics say
articles.summary: More than 25 people remain trapped in Red Sea state amid limited rescue resources
articles.context: The collapse of the gold mine in eastern Sudan highlights the ongoing challenges in the region, exacerbated by the 2021 military coup that destabilized the transitional government. The Red Sea state has seen increased informal mining activities as economic conditions worsen, with groups like the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces vying for control over lucrative resources. This incident underscores the dire need for improved safety regulations and humanitarian support in conflict-affected areas.

articles.title: 'Don’t forget Sudan': African Union chair presses for ceasefire push
articles.summary: 'Sudan is passing through disastrous situation,' Mahmoud Ali Youssouf tells Anadolu, adding that lack of media coverage and shifting attention to Mideast conflict is leading to concerns
articles.context: Sudan has been embroiled in conflict since the ousting of President Omar al-Bashir in April 2019, leading to a power struggle between military and civilian factions. The recent outbreak of violence in April 2023 between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces has exacerbated the humanitarian crisis, displacing millions. The African Union's call for a ceasefire comes amid concerns that the international community's focus is shifting to the Israel-Palestine conflict, sidelining Sudan's urgent needs.

articles.title: Sudan slams Germany-hosted conference as ‘colonial tutelage approach’
articles.summary: Foreign Ministry says hosting conference without consulting Khartoum reflects ‘disregard’ for Sudan
articles.context: Sudan has been grappling with political instability since the ousting of President Omar al-Bashir in April 2019, leading to a power struggle between military and civilian factions. The ongoing conflict in Darfur and the recent coup in October 2021 have further complicated the situation. The German-hosted conference, aimed at addressing Sudan's transition and international support, has drawn criticism from Khartoum, which perceives it as undermining its sovereignty and disregarding its role in the peace process.

articles.title: Three years of messages at once - a chronicle of Sudan's war pours in as trapped reporter's phone turns on
articles.summary: As the conflict enters its fourth year, journalist Mohamed Suleiman shudders at what has been lost.
articles.context: Since the outbreak of conflict in Sudan in April 2023, following a power struggle between the Sudanese Armed Forces led by General Abdel Fattah al-Burhan and the Rapid Support Forces commanded by General Mohamed Hamdan Dagalo, the country has descended into chaos. The war has resulted in widespread displacement, humanitarian crises, and a breakdown of communication, making the messages from trapped journalist Mohamed Suleiman particularly poignant as they reflect the dire realities faced by civilians.

articles.title: The human cost of the war in Sudan, three years on
articles.summary: The conflict, which erupted in 2023, has left behind a human toll which is "simply staggering", reports the BBC's Barbara Plett Usher.
articles.context: The war in Sudan began in April 2023, primarily between the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the Rapid Support Forces (RSF), following a power struggle between General Abdel Fattah al-Burhan and General Mohamed Hamdan Dagalo. The conflict has exacerbated existing humanitarian crises, with over 5 million people displaced and widespread food insecurity. The ongoing violence undermines fragile peace agreements from the 2020 Juba Peace Agreement, complicating efforts for stability in the region.

articles.title: FACTBOX - 3 years of war: The human cost in Sudan
articles.summary: Sudan’s civil war has uprooted nearly 15M people and killed over an estimated 150,000 as the conflict enters its 4th year with over 33M in need of humanitarian aid and 21M facing a deepening food crisis
articles.context: Since the outbreak of conflict in April 2021 between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces, the struggle for power has exacerbated existing ethnic tensions and economic instability. The 2019 ousting of President Omar al-Bashir, following months of protests, set the stage for a fragile transition that ultimately collapsed, leading to widespread violence and humanitarian crises. The ongoing clashes have hindered peace efforts and complicated regional relations, particularly with neighboring South Sudan and Egypt.

articles.title: Fire at Sudan’s Darfur displacement camp leaves 350 families homeless
articles.summary: Blaze in South Darfur camp destroys shelters and forces hundreds of families to relocate amid worsening humanitarian conditions
articles.context: The fire at the displacement camp in South Darfur comes amid escalating violence in the region, particularly following the outbreak of conflict in April 2023 between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces. This ongoing conflict has exacerbated humanitarian crises, displacing millions and straining resources. The situation is further complicated by the 2020 Juba Peace Agreement, which has failed to bring lasting stability, leaving vulnerable populations like those in Darfur in dire need of assistance.

articles.title: Africa harder hit by Iran war than Germany, says Chancellor Merz
articles.summary: Africa faces looming inflation and food crisis, warns German leader ahead of conference on civil war in Sudan
articles.context: The ongoing civil war in Sudan, which escalated in April 2023 between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces, has led to widespread violence and displacement. This conflict has disrupted agricultural production, exacerbating food insecurity across Africa. As the war continues, the ripple effects threaten to drive inflation and food crises in neighboring countries, prompting urgent discussions at the upcoming international conference on Sudan's humanitarian situation.

articles.title: Somali forces kill 27 al-Shabaab terrorists in 'large-scale operation': Defense Ministry
articles.summary: Key members of terrorist group killed, weapons, military equipment seized
articles.context: The operation comes amid intensified efforts by the Somali government, supported by African Union forces, to combat al-Shabaab, which has been waging an insurgency since 2006. The recent offensive aligns with the Somali National Army's "Operation Dhuulow," launched in August 2023, aimed at reclaiming territory and dismantling the group's influence ahead of the 2024 elections. This military action is crucial for stabilizing the region and restoring public confidence in the government.

articles.title: Red Cross warns of worsening Sudan crisis as displacement shifts
articles.summary: ‘The needs are overwhelming. Sudan cannot be forgotten’ says secretary-general of Sudanese Red Crescent
articles.context: Sudan has been engulfed in conflict since April 2023, following a power struggle between the Sudanese Armed Forces and the Rapid Support Forces, leading to widespread violence and humanitarian crises. Over 5 million people have been displaced, exacerbating an already dire situation stemming from years of political instability and economic hardship. The recent ceasefire agreements, including the Jeddah Declaration in May 2023, have failed to bring lasting peace, leaving millions in urgent need of assistance.

articles.title: الذكرى الثالثة للحرب في السودان: رسائل لن تصل
articles.summary: تشير تقديرات ميدانية وتحليلية شاملة لمشروع "ACLED" والتحقيقات الصحفية الدولية (مثل نيويورك تايمز) المنشورة في مطلع عام 2026 إلى أن الرقم الفعلي للضحايا تجاوز 150,000 قتيل عند احتساب الوفيات الناتجة عن الجوع والمرض وانهيار النظام الصحي.
articles.context: تحتفل السودان في عام 2026 بالذكرى الثالثة للحرب التي اندلعت في أبريل 2023 بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع، مما أدى إلى تفاقم الأزمات الإنسانية. تدهور الوضع الأمني والسياسي بعد الإطاحة بالرئيس عمر البشير في 2019، حيث تتصارع الفصائل المختلفة على السلطة. تشير التقارير إلى أن الصراع قد أسفر عن وفاة أكثر من 150,000 شخص، مما يعكس حجم الكارثة الإنسانية المستمرة.

articles.title: Wildberries Opens its E-commerce Platform to Sellers in Ethiopia
articles.summary: Eurasia’s digital marketplace Wildberries has expanded operations into Ethiopia, with the opening of its e-commerce platform to sellers in the East African country. The Wildberries marketplace will feature
articles.context: Ethiopia's e-commerce landscape has been evolving, particularly following the 2020 Tigray conflict, which disrupted traditional markets and pushed many businesses online. The government's push for digital transformation, highlighted by the 2021 Digital Ethiopia 2025 strategy, aims to foster economic growth and attract foreign investment. Wildberries' entry into this market aligns with these efforts, potentially providing a platform for local entrepreneurs amid ongoing economic challenges and a need for diversified income sources.

articles.context: Wegagen Bank's partnership with the IFC comes at a critical time as Ethiopia seeks to stabilize its economy amid ongoing challenges, including the aftermath of the Tigray conflict and rising inflation. The agreement, signed in October 2023, aims to strengthen trade financing in a recovering economic landscape.

articles.context: The Dangote Group, led by Nigerian billionaire Aliko Dangote, has been pivotal in driving industrial growth in Africa, particularly through investments in cement and agriculture. In Ethiopia, the government has been focusing on economic reforms and attracting foreign investment since the 2018 peace agreement with Eritrea, which has opened up new opportunities for regional trade. Afreximbank's support aligns with Ethiopia's Vision 2025 plan to enhance industrialization and economic resilience amid ongoing challenges in the Horn of Africa.

articles.title: كيف يصنع الشباب اللبناني الأمل في زمن الحرب؟
articles.summary: يشهد لبنان منذ أكثر من شهر جولة جديدة من الحرب، تترافق مع موجات نزوح جماعية، ودمار واسع، وأزمة إنسانية تثقل كاهل اللبنانيين.
articles.context: لبنان يعاني من أزمة متعددة الأبعاد منذ اندلاع الحرب الأهلية في 1975، حيث تفاقمت الأوضاع مع الانهيار الاقتصادي في 2019 واندلاع الاحتجاجات ضد الفساد. في أكتوبر 2023، تصاعدت التوترات مع الهجمات على الحدود اللبنانية الإسرائيلية، مما أدى إلى موجات نزوح جديدة. الشباب اللبناني، في ظل هذه الظروف، يسعون لإيجاد الأمل من خلال مبادرات مجتمعية لمواجهة التحديات الإنسانية والاقتصادية.

articles.title: Ethiopia’s Trailblazing Diplomat Konjit Sinegiorgis Dies
articles.summary: Ambassador Konjit Sinegiorgis, a pioneering figure in Ethiopia’s foreign service and one of Africa’s longest-serving women diplomats, has died at the age of 86. The Ministry of Foreign
articles.context: Ambassador Konjit Sinegiorgis played a crucial role in shaping Ethiopia's foreign policy during a period marked by significant political upheaval, including the 1991 overthrow of the Derg regime and the subsequent establishment of a federal system. Her contributions were particularly vital during the 2018 peace agreement with Eritrea, which ended a two-decade stalemate. As Ethiopia navigates ongoing internal conflicts and regional tensions, her legacy as a diplomat underscores the importance of women in leadership roles in diplomacy.

articles.title: قائد الدعم السريع يجدد القبول بهدنة إنسانية «جادة»
articles.summary: الخرطوم، 20 مارس 2026 – جدد قائد قوات الدعم السريع محمد حمدان دقلو حميدتي، الجمعة، موافقته الصريحة على هدنة جادة وذات مصداقية للأغراض الإنسانية الخالصة. وقال حميدتي في كلمة له بمناسبة عيد الفطر إن الهدنة الإنسانية يجب أن تكفل وصول المساعدات إلى المدنيين بلا عوائق، وفتح الممرات الآمنة وتخفيف معاناة المتضررين وحماية النازحين واللاجئين. وأضاف قائد الدعم السريع أن تنفيذ الهدنة يجب ان يضمن عدم استغلالها لمآرب عسكرية أو سياسية ضيقة – حسب تعبيره -. وكان قائد الجيش السوداني عبد الفتاح البرهان قد جدد أمس رفضه لأي هدنة أو وقف إطلاق نار قبل أن تتجمع قوات الدعم السريع في مناطق بعينها وتسلم أسلحتها.

articles.title: «التعليم العالي» في السودان تنفي استيلاءها على أموال الجامعات
articles.summary: الخرطوم 18 مارس 2026 – نفت وزارة التعليم العالي والبحث العلمي في السودان، الأربعاء، مزاعم استيلائها على جزء من الدعم الإضافي الذي خصصته وزارة المالية للجامعات. وتحدث ناشطون في مواقع التواصل الاجتماعي عن استيلاء الوزارة على 40% من الدعم المؤسسي الذي قدمته وزارة المالية للجامعات. وخصصت وزارة المالية دعماً إضافياً للجامعات في إطار موازنة العام الحالي، لتحسين الوضع المالي لأعضاء هيئة التدريس والكوادر المساعدة وتأهيلهم وتدريبهم، على أن يُصرف حسب المعايير التي توصي بها لجنة التمويل ويجيزها المجلس القومي للتعليم العالي والبحث العلمي. وقالت وزارة التعليم العالي، في بيان، إن “الدعم الإضافي ما زال محفوظاً ولن يتم التصرف فيه لحين إكمال قاعدة بيانات...

articles.title: تشاد تتوعد بالرد بعد هجوم لمسيّرة سودانية على «الطينة»
articles.summary: الطينة 18 مارس 2026 لقي 16 مدنياً تشادياً على الأقل حتفهم، وأصيب آخرون إثر هجوم شنته طائرة مسيّرة يُرجح تبعيتها للدعم السريع، استهدف تجمعاً للمواطنين في بلدة “مبروكة” التابعة لبلدة الطينة في إقليم “وادي فيرا” التشادي، فيما وجه الرئيس التشادي بالرد على أي هجوم قادم من السودان. ويعد هذا الهجوم الرابع من نوعه خلال الأشهر الأخيرة، بعد أن توغلت قوة من الدعم السريع في يناير الماضي داخل الأراضي التشادية واستهدفت معسكراً للجيش، مما أسفر عن مقتل ثمانية جنود. وأعلنت الدعم السريع وقتها أن الهجوم وقع عن طريق الخطأ وقدمت اعتذارها للحكومة التشادية. ويتزامن هجوم اليوم الأربعاء مع المواجهات العسكرية الضارية التي تشهدها “الطينة” هذا الأسبوع، حيث يتصدى...

articles.title: «أدري».. من شريان حياة إلى بوابة مؤصدة: جحيم الحصار يطارد نازحي دارفور
articles.summary: أدري 18 مارس 2026- أصدرت الحكومة التشادية في فبراير الماضي قراراً فاجأ آلاف النازحين السودانيين العالقين على حدودها الشرقية، بإعلانها إغلاق جميع المعابر الحدودية مع السودان “حتى إشعار آخر”. وكان منفذ “أدري”، الذي يعد الشريان الرئيسي الذي يعبره الفارون من جحيم الحرب في دارفور، أول المغلَقين. لم يكن القرار تشادياً خالصاً، بل جاء تتويجاً لأسابيع من التوتر المتصاعد على الحدود، أدت إلى سقوط قتلى في صفوف الجنود التشاديين. وبين ليلة وضحاها، تحول معبر الأمل إلى بوابة مؤصدة، تاركاً مئات العائلات عالقة في العراء، بين جحيم الحرب خلفها وبوابة مغلقة أمامها.

articles.title: أطفال السودان في رمضان.. طفولة سرقتها الحرب
articles.summary: منتدى الإعلام السوداني بورتسودان، مدني، 18 مارس 2026 (سودان تربيون)– لم تكن آمنة محمد تتخيل أن يأتي رمضان يومًا وهي تحاول إقناع أطفالها بأن القليل من الطعام يكفي للإفطار. نزحت آمنة مع أطفالها الثلاثة من منزلهم في الخرطوم قبل ثلاث سنوات، بعد اشتداد القتال بين الجيش وقوات الدعم السريع، لتجد نفسها في أحد مراكز الإيواء بمدينة بورتسودان، حيث تغيرت ملامح الشهر الكريم تمامًا بالنسبة لأطفالها. تقول آمنة بصوت يختلط فيه الحزن بالصبر: “كان أطفالي ينتظرون رمضان كل عام لشراء الفوانيس والملابس الجديدة، لكن هذا العام لا نفكر إلا في توفير الطعام. أحيانًا لا نجد ما يكفي للإفطار.

articles.title: ايصال أدوية لدعم (80) ألف طفل في «طويلة» غرب السودان
articles.summary: طويلة 18 مارس 2026 – أعلنت منظمة أنقذوا الأطفال، الأربعاء، عن إيصال أدوية لدعم 80 ألف طفل و57 ألف بالغ لمدة 6 أشهر في طويلة بولاية شمال دارفور غربي السودان. وتحوّلت طويلة، الخاضعة لسيطرة حركة تحرير السودان بقيادة عبد الواحد نور، من بلدات نائية تفتقر إلى الخدمات إلى مأوى لأكثر من 715 ألف نازح، معظمهم فرّوا من الهجمات على الفاشر ومخيمات النزوح المجاورة لها. وقالت المنظمة، في بيان، إنها أوصلت “شحنة من بورتسودان إلى طويلة لدعم أكثر من 80 ألف طفل و57 ألف بالغ في طويلة، حيث إنها كافية للإبقاء على عمل 20 مرفقًا صحيًا وعيادات متنقلة تخدم المجتمعات النازحة لمدة ستة أشهر”. وأشارت إلى أن الشحنة، التي تحمل 30 طنًا من الأدوية وإمدادات التغذية، وصلت...

articles.title: شبكة أطباء السودان تتهم «الدعم السريع »بتصفية مدنيين قرب بارا
articles.summary: بارا 18 مارس 2026 – قالت شبكة أطباء السودان، الأربعاء، إن قوات الدعم السريع اغتالت 12 مدنيا بينهم نساء وأصابت 3 آخرين بذريعة الانتماء للجيش، في منطقة (شريم ميما) شمالي مدينة بارا بولاية شمال كردفان. وتبادل كل من الجيش السوداني والدعم السريع السيطرة على بارا ثاني أكبر مدن ولاية شمال كردفان عدة مرات، قبل أن تسيطر عليها قوات الدعم السريع هذا الأسبوع. وأفادت الشبكة في بيان، إن “الدعم السريع ارتكبت مجزرة جديدة في منطقة شريم ميما شمالي مدينة بارا، حيث صفّت 12 شخصا بينهم 6 نساء، وأصابت 3 آخرين بجروح متفاوتة، بتهمة انتمائهم إلى الجيش السوداني”. وأشارت الشبكة إلى أن استهداف المدنيين العزّل يمثل انتهاكا صارخا للقوانين والأعراف الإنسانية والدولية.

articles.summary: النهود 17 مارس 2026- أقدمت قوات الدعم السريع، الثلاثاء، على إحراق قافلة تجارية تضم شاحنات كبيرة محملة بمحاصيل زراعية بالقرب من مدينة النهود بولاية غرب كردفان، أثناء توجهها إلى الأبيض بولاية شمال كردفان. ووثق عناصر من القوات عبر مقطع فيديو لحظة إضرام النار في الشاحنات التي كانت محملة بكميات كبيرة من المحاصيل الزراعية، بما في ذلك الذرة والفول السوداني. وتُعد النهود وغبيش وودبندة بولاية غرب كردفان من أبرز مناطق إنتاج الفول في السودان، كما تضم النهود واحدة من أكبر بورصات تداول المحاصيل في غرب السودان، غير أنها تعرضت لعمليات نهب واسعة عقب سيطرة الدعم السريع على المنطقة، الأمر الذي أدى إلى اضطراب النشاط التجاري ودفع العديد من التجار إلى مغادرة...

articles.title: السودان : معدل التضخم يواصل التراجع
articles.summary: الخرطوم17 مارس 2026 – كشف جهاز الإحصاء المركزي في السودان، الثلاثاء، عن تراجع معدل التضخم في فبراير 2026 إلى 56% مقارنة بالشهر الذي سبقه. وبدأ معدل التضخم ينخفض تدريجيًا بعد أن بلغ أعلى مستوياته 422% في يوليو 2021، رغم الانخفاض المتوالي في قيمة العملة المحلية مقابل العملات الأخرى وارتفاع أسعار السلع، حيث سجل 60.26% في يناير الماضي. وقال جهاز الإحصاء، في بيان، إن “معدل التغير في المستوى العام للأسعار – التضخم – في شهر فبراير 2026 زاد بنسبة 56.39% عن نظيره في العام السابق”. وأشار إلى أن معدل التضخم في المناطق الحضرية في شهر فبراير بلغ 72.12%، فيما وصل معدله في المناطق الريفية إلى 54.08%.

articles.title: «بنك السودان»: عجز الميزان التجاري في 2025 بلغ 3.8 مليار دولار
articles.summary: الخرطوم، 16 مارس 2026 – كشف بنك السودان المركزي، الاثنين، عن اتساع العجز في الميزان التجاري للسودان خلال العام الماضي إلى 3.8 مليار دولار. وظل السودان يسجل عجزًا في الميزان التجاري كل عام، يتراوح بين 3 إلى 6 مليارات دولار، قبل أن ينخفض في 2024 إلى 1.7 مليار دولار. وقال البنك المركزي، في تقرير الموجز الإحصائي للتجارة الخارجية لعام 2025، إن “أجمالي الصادرات السودانية بلغ نحو 2.64 مليار دولار، مقابل واردات بلغت 6.49 مليار دولار، مما أدى إلى تسجيل عجز تجاري قدره نحو 3.86 مليار دولار”. وأوضح أن قيمة الصادرات البالغة 2.639 مليار دولار جاءت نتيجة هيمنة قطاع المعادن والمنتجات الزراعية والثروة الحيوانية.

articles.title: «الدعم السريع» تسيطر على بارا بشمال كردفان وكرنوي بشمال دارفور
articles.summary: بارا، 16 مارس 2026 – سيطرت قوات الدعم السريع مرة أخرى، الإثنين، على مدينة بارا ثاني أكبر مدن ولاية شمال كردفان وعلى بلدة كرنوي الحدودية مع تشاد بولاية شمال دارفور. وتقع بارا على بعد نحو 45 كيلومتر شرقي الأبيض عاصمة الولاية، بينما تقع كرنوي ضمن آخر المعاقل الإثنية للقوة المشتركة للحركات التي تقاتل إلى جانب الجيش السوداني. ومنذ اندلاع الحرب بين الجيش السوداني وقوات الدعم السريع في أبريل 2023 سيطرت قوات الدعم السريع على مدينة بارا، ومن ثم استعادها الجيش في سبتمبر 2025. لكن قوات الدعم السريع سيطرت على المدينة مجددا في أكتوبر من العام الماضي، ثم استعادها الجيش قبل 10 أيام، قبل أن تعلن قوات الدعم السريع اليوم الإثنين الاستيلاء على بارا.

articles.summary: الخرطوم 15 مارس 2026 – اعتقلت السلطات في السودان، الأحد، القيادي الإسلامي البارز الناجي عبد الله، وفقاً لما أفاد به أحد قادة المقاومة الشعبية. وظل الناجي عبد الله وهو أحد قيادات المقاومة الشعبية التي تقاتل إلى جانب الجيش في حربه ضد قوات الدعم السريع يجاهر بمواقفه المؤيدة للقضية الفلسطينية وداعماً لحركة المقاومة الإسلامية “حماس”. وأعلن الناجي في الثالث من مارس الجاري، خلال مخاطبته إفطاراً رمضانياً أمام حشد من مجموعات عسكرية محسوبة على التيار الإسلامي، دعمهم لإيران في الحرب التي تقودها ضد إسرائيل والولايات المتحدة الأميركية. وأثارت تلك التصريحات ردود فعل واسعة حيث أعلن القائد العام للجيش عبد الفتاح البرهان تبرؤه من تلك المجموعة، مؤكداً...

articles.title: «كبر» يكذب صحة مقطع فيديو يتحدث عن «البرهان» أثار جدلًا واسعًا
articles.summary: الخرطوم 16 مارس 2026 – نفى رئيس شورى المؤتمر الوطني – المحلول، والي شمال دارفور الأسبق عثمان محمد يوسف كبر، فجر الاثنين، صحة مقطع فيديو أثار جدلًا واسعًا في السودان تحدث خلاله عن رئيس مجلس السيادة وقائد الجيش عبد الفتاح البرهان. ونشرت قناة “اسكاي نيوز عربية” الإماراتية، الأحد، تقريرًا حوى مقطع فيديو يظهر كبر جالسًا على كرسي مريح يتحدث إلى أشخاص قربه في جلسة يبدو أنها غير رسمية، دون أن تُظهر اللقطات هؤلاء الأشخاص الجالسين قربه. وقال كبر، في مقطع الفيديو، إنهم “تحدثوا للبرهان لتسلميه المجلس العسكري لسحق المتظاهرين وتسليم السلطة إلى الإسلاميين، وهو لم يكن له دور، وما كان محسوبًا ضابطًا، حيث كان يجلس مثل الحرس خارج الباب”. وقال كبر، في بيان،...

articles.title: الصين تعلن  الاستعداد لمساعدة السودان في إعادة الإعمار
articles.summary: بورتسودان 15 مارس 2026 – أعلن القائم بأعمال السفارة الصينية في السودان شيوي جيان، الأحد، وضع مقترحات لمساعدة الخرطوم في إعادة الإعمار ضمن سياسات بكين الخارجية. وتعرضت مدن عديدة، بما في ذلك العاصمة الخرطوم، لدمار واسع طال المؤسسات الخدمية مثل المياه والكهرباء والصحة، إضافة إلى البنية التحتية والمصانع، علاوة على تأثير النزاع على الأنشطة الزراعية والثروة الحيوانية. وقال شيوي جيان، في تنوير صحفي بمدينة بورتسودان شرقي البلاد، إن الصين “على استعداد للمساهمة في إعمار ما دمرته الحرب بالسودان”. وأشار إلى أن بكين ضمنت مقترحات بشأن هذه المساهمة في سياساتها الخارجية للفترة المقبلة.
